summary: Matched the previously stubbed `ifStatus_802F491C` as `ifStatus_PercentOnDeathAnimationThink`, a HUD damage-percent death animation update routine. The PR replaced the address-style symbol in `symbols.txt` and assembly callsites, implemented the function in C with JObj translation/dirty-matrix handling, random velocity initialization, assert-preserving helper code, and moved related ifstatus data out of asm. It also cleaned HUD damage types by renaming `HudValue`/`HudFlags` to `IfDamageState`/`IfDamageFlags`, adding an `IfDamagePlace` enum for hundreds/tens/ones/percent slots, and replacing individual digit fields with indexed arrays.
what_changed: `ifStatus_802F491C` was renamed to `ifStatus_PercentOnDeathAnimationThink` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` and in the remaining assembly callsite in `asm/melee/if/ifstatus.s`.

`src/melee/if/ifstatus.c` replaced the `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` body with a C implementation that updates four HUD percent-token JObjs, initializes randomized x/y velocities when a flag is set, applies per-frame translation, marks matrices dirty, and applies a downward velocity decrement.

The matched function uses `HSD_Randf`, `fabsf_bitwise`, `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub`, and preserved JObj assertions with literal file/string evidence (`jobj.h`, `jobj`) and line numbers such as 993, 1006, 1102, and 1114.
`ifStatus_PercentOnDeathAnimationThink` was made `static` in C and removed from `ifstatus.h`, indicating it is an internal think callback rather than exported API.
`asm/melee/if/ifstatus_data.s` was deleted, with related data/static globals now represented from C-side declarations in `ifstatus.c` and type definitions.
`HudFlags` became `IfDamageFlags`; `HudValue` became `IfDamageState`; and `unk40` was renamed to `randomize_velocity` at existing use sites.
`IfDamagePlace` was introduced with `Hundreds`, `Tens`, `Ones`, `Percent`, and `HUD_PLACE_MAX`, allowing digit/percent-sign state to be represented as arrays indexed by place.
`IfDamageState` fields were compacted from individually named digit members into arrays: `translation_x`, `translation_y`, `velocity_x`, `velocity_y`, and `jobjs`, all sized by `HUD_PLACE_MAX`.
`Element_803F9628` fields were renamed from `unk0`, `unk4`, etc. to offset-style names such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, and `xC`, and an affected cleanup path in `ifStatus_802F7220` was updated accordingly.
`src/melee/it/items/itcerebi.c` received small nonfunctional cleanup around static declaration ordering and a trailing comma in `ItemStateTable`.
smart_moves: Used a domain name, `PercentOnDeathAnimationThink`, for the matched function instead of leaving the address-derived `ifStatus_802F491C`, while keeping the address comment and symbol map evidence.
Modeled repeated digit/percent-sign state as arrays plus an `IfDamagePlace` enum, reducing brittle per-field duplication and matching the loop over four JObjs in the target code.
Kept the matched routine file-local by declaring it `static` and removing the public header declaration, which avoids advertising an internal callback as API.
Used small inline helpers for JObj translation, matrix-dirty behavior, and assertion sites to steer codegen while keeping recognizable C structure.
Preserved assert strings and line numbers through an `ASSERT_NOT_NULL` macro, which is important for matching functions that inline HSD JObj assertion behavior.
Used an sbss toggle (`ifStatus_804D6D61`) to reproduce alternating positive/negative randomized x velocities for percent-token explosion movement.
Carried constant evidence into comments, including `100.0f`, `-100.0f`, `0.6083f`, `0.3041f`, `0.811f`, `1.2165f`, and `0.2028f`, making future refinement easier.
decomp_lessons: When a HUD struct has repeated fields for hundreds/tens/ones/percent, an enum-indexed array can both improve names and match loops over four elements.
For JObj-heavy matches, helper functions may need to be `inline` and shaped around exact assert and dirty-matrix call patterns rather than purely idiomatic C.
Do not export matched functions unnecessarily; if an assembly routine is only used as an internal callback, a `static` C declaration plus symbol-map name can be enough.
Migrating data out of `*_data.s` can be coupled with a function match, but only when the C declarations and section/static storage reproduce the original layout.
Bitfield names can encode behavioral discoveries from a match; here `unk40` becoming `randomize_velocity` was supported by the random-velocity initialization path.
Offset-style field names such as `x0`/`x4` are preferable to misleading `unk0` names when the exact semantics are still unknown but offset stability matters.
The evidence for local helper names like `foo`, `jobj_unk_x`, `jobj_unk_y`, and temporary `UnkX` is weak; they appear to be matching scaffolding rather than final semantic names.
naming_conventions: `ifStatus_802F491C` -> `ifStatus_PercentOnDeathAnimationThink` for a newly matched ifstatus HUD death-animation think routine.
`HudValue` -> `IfDamageState`, naming the per-player HUD damage display state rather than a generic value.
`HudFlags` -> `IfDamageFlags`, naming the bitfield group by subsystem and role.
`unk40` -> `randomize_velocity`, based on the flag triggering random x/y velocity initialization.
`IfDamagePlace` enum values use display-place terms: `Hundreds`, `Tens`, `Ones`, `Percent`, with `HUD_PLACE_MAX` as the array bound.
Repeated place-specific fields use array names with axis/role suffixes: `translation_x`, `translation_y`, `velocity_x`, `velocity_y`, and `jobjs`.
For still-unknown structs, offset-style names like `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, and `x10` replaced older `unk0`/`unk4` style in `Element_803F9628`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed the assembly label and symbol map entry while matching the function in C, keeping address `0x802F491C` and size `0x268` stable.
Used inline JObj helpers (`jobj_translate_x`, `jobj_translate_y`, `jobj_flagCheckSetMtxDirtySub`) to reproduce instruction ordering around translation updates and dirty-matrix propagation.
Preserved HSD assertion behavior with `__assert("jobj.h", line, "jobj")` and exact line constants to match inlined JObj accessors.
Used `fabsf_bitwise(jobj->translate.x) < 100.0f` and `translate.y > -100.0f` gates to mirror float comparisons from the assembly.
Used `(&value->x34_vec.x)[i]` and `(&value->x44_vec.x)[i]` indexing over `Vec4` storage to match array-like access to four x/y velocity lanes.
Introduced a seemingly artificial `jobj_get` helper and repeated calls/casts in the y-path, likely to force the same reload/register behavior as the target assembly.
Preserved global/static storage for `ifStatus_803F9628`, `ifStatus_804D6D60`, and `ifStatus_804D6D61`, with `ifStatus_804D6D61` controlling alternating random sign generation.
Kept unused parameters in `ifStatus_PercentOnDeathAnimationThink(UnkX* value, s32 arg1, s32 arg2)` to match the callback ABI even though only `value` is used in the shown implementation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states `Resolves #533, resolves #683`; no reviewer rationale or requested changes are available.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for replacement of temporary local names `UnkX`, `FlagsX`, `foo`, `jobj_unk_x`, and `jobj_unk_y` with semantic HUD damage names.
Check follow-up ifstatus matches around `ifStatus_802F4B84`, `ifStatus_802F4EDC`, and `ifStatus_802F5B48` for reuse of `IfDamageState` and `IfDamagePlace`.
Audit whether the deleted `ifstatus_data.s` data was fully represented in C after this PR or refined in later data-migration PRs.
Look for subsequent review or commits around issue #533 and #683 to confirm the intended semantics of `PercentOnDeathAnimationThink` and `randomize_velocity`.
Search for additional HUD percent digit animation code that can reuse enum-indexed arrays for hundreds/tens/ones/percent slots.
